name: username
description: Open, horizontal, re-usable Claude Code skills & plugins by Username (Studio A.I.).

plugins.name: claude-remote-spawn
plugins.description: Spawn or resume a persistent, remote-controllable Claude Code session you can drive from your phone or desktop. PTY-backed, survives detached. Terminal-agnostic, cross-platform. Pick the session model with --model (any alias/id your claude accepts, passed straight to claude --model).

plugins.name: aws-remote-auth
plugins.description: On-demand AWS SSO re-authentication via device code (autofill link), with a PreToolUse hook that auto-detects expired sessions on aws commands. Generic across profiles and SSO portals.

plugins.name: delivery-metrics
plugins.description: Generate a developer productivity & quality report (interactive HTML dashboard) from git history — single repo or submodule workspace. Repo-agnostic and configurable.

plugins.name: coding-agent-usage
plugins.description: Diagnose your own Claude Code usage and benchmark it against Anthropic's published per-developer figures. Parses local transcripts into cost, tokens, sessions, model mix, tool distribution, cache efficiency, thinking and subagent rates, per-project and per-week breakdowns, and renders an interactive HTML dashboard with a log-scale percentile gauge. Benchmark figures are fetched live and cached 24h. Includes a multi-provider view (Claude + Codex + Gemini via ccusage). Cost is computed from a maintained price table and the report flags any tokens billed at a fallback rate (unrecognized model family), so accuracy is auditable rather than assumed. No account access.

plugins.name: find-session
plugins.description: Find and resume a past Claude Code session from a search term — decomposes the query, cross-matches local transcripts, and returns the best session ID with a ready claude --resume command. Project-aware, macOS and Linux.

plugins.name: security-audit
plugins.description: Full Trivy security audit — dependency CVEs across every ecosystem, secrets, and IaC misconfigurations — as a prioritised Markdown report and a dark HTML dashboard. Report-only, language-agnostic, Trivy the only dependency.

plugins.name: ship-when-done
plugins.description: A Stop-hook harness that commits at each milestone (the commit is the anti-loss), pushes the feature branch, and opens a draft PR/MR once the work is provably done — the goal's checklist satisfied AND the project's quality gate actually green. If a sibling merge-review gate is active, it HOLDS the push until that review passes the current HEAD — the quality gate runs before anything reaches the remote — and continues your session to run it. Forge-agnostic (GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket): uses gh/glab if present, else GitLab push options, else surfaces the PR-creation URL — no CLI dependency. Branch-first, never commits or pushes the default branch, never merges, no AI attribution. Engages on its own only on work this session produced (never a pre-existing dirty tree); opt out per repo.

plugins.name: mr-watchdog
plugins.description: Triggered by an open merge request, mr-watchdog watches the MR's remote CI as a background task your MAIN session owns: the watcher is launched with run_in_background and tracked by the harness, which re-invokes your session the moment the pipeline resolves — so the verdict reaches you IN the conversation (no detached daemon, no status file, no polling). Strictly read-only — never commits, pushes, or merges. Green → 'ok, all good'; red → it hands back the failing job log so your session fixes the ROOT cause (no bypass), with a verify command to self-check the fix for fake-green (deleted/weakened tests, --no-verify, || true, lowered thresholds). A Stop hook nudges the session to launch the watcher, once per pipeline HEAD. Engages on its own only for a branch this session pushed (never a stale or someone else's MR); opt out per repo. Forge-agnostic (GitHub via gh, GitLab via glab). The CI-watch step after ship-when-done → merge-review.

plugins.name: merge-review
plugins.description: Adversarial merge-readiness review that scores a diff 0-100 across security, correctness, quality and maintainability and runs iteratively until the score clears the threshold (default 80). Two outcome modes: LOCAL (interactive) re-derives each finding, applies the attested ones (high-confidence, file:line-cited) as minimal root-cause fixes and loops until viable — contestable pure-logic findings are surfaced for you to arbitrate, never silently applied; REMOTE (diff injected by a CI/bot runner) is strictly read-only and just emits the verdict + machine-readable state. An asymmetric trust model treats diff, descriptions and comments as untrusted data that may only raise scrutiny, never lower the verdict. Self-engaging & session-scoped: a pre-push gate reviews the branch THIS session is about to push (never a stale or someone else's); a fake-green guard blocks deleted/weakened tests, --no-verify, || true, lowered thresholds; opt out per repo. Forge-agnostic (gh/glab). The review step between ship-when-done and mr-watchdog.

plugins.name: proof-of-fix
plugins.description: Evidence-first bug fixing: prove the bug BEFORE touching code (record runs the probe and accepts it only if it FAILS), fix the root cause, then prove the fix with the SAME probe (check passes only when it runs green) — red before, green after, same command. Auto-engaging: a bug-shaped prompt (en/fr) injects the protocol into context once per session per repo, and a Stop hook re-runs an open repro itself — auto-closing it on green, blocking with the failing output on red (one attempt per work-state, capped — never a Stop loop). The best probe is a real test committed with the fix, so the repro becomes a permanent regression guard. State in .git, never committed; opt out per repo. Horizontal: any repo, any stack — git + Python stdlib only.
